title: Cleistes stricta
uses: Cleistes stricta, also known as the "Green Bird's Nest," is a species of orchid that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal uses are primarily based on its purported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to be used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption. Traditional healers have also used Cleistes stricta to treat wounds and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cleistes stricta is classified in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Cleistes stricta, specifically, is a genus of orchids native to South America. The genus Cleistes is further classified within the subfamily Vanilloideae. It is a small genus of terrestrial orchids that are often found in grasslands and savannas.

However, the exact mechanisms and health benefits of Cleistes stricta require further scientific investigation.

intro: Cleistes stricta, also known as the tongue orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents found in Cleistes stricta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Cleistes stricta include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
